summary: node-procmetrics postinstall installs a remote command-execution backdoor
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall script (install.js, referenced by package.json scripts.postinstall) decodes a hex-encoded byte array via String.fromCharCode(parseInt(h,16)) to reconstruct the C2 URL http://152.53.120.90/cmd, writes a persistent bash polling loop to /tmp/.sysm.sh, chmod +x's it, and spawns it detached with stdio ignored and unref(). The loop repeatedly fetches shell commands from http://152.53.120.90/cmd/commands, executes each via bash on the installing user's machine, and POSTs stdout and exit codes back to http://152.53.120.90/cmd/results. The C2 destination is a bare IP over plain HTTP and is obfuscated to evade string scanning. This is a full remote-command-execution backdoor with persistence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
